tutorial.title: Masonry Tutorial
tutorial.subtitle: Knap a chisel, build the bench, batch the stone

objectives.label: Knap a Stone Chisel
substeps: Research Masonry at the settlement Campfire.
Knap two rocks together and shape the Stone Chisel — the mason's tool.

objectives.label: Form a Mason's Bench
substeps: Place two Stone or Cobblestone blocks side by side.
Right-click one of them with the Stone Chisel to lash up the bench.

objectives.label: Open the build menu
substeps: Right-click the Mason's Bench with base stone to set a budget.
Choose a build list (slabs, stairs, walls, bricks, polished).
Right-click with the Chisel and run the strike step — no quality, just throughput.

pages.text: &6Masonry&r is to stone what carpentry is to wood. At the Mason's Bench you set a stone budget and a build list, then run a chisel-strike minigame to dress rough stone into slabs, stairs, walls, bricks, and polished blocks in batches.

The chisel step is a non-skill step — there is no quality on masonry output — so it is about throughput, not craftsmanship. The Stone Chisel is the mason's tool: knapped by hand, kept in the workshop's storage to staff it, and used to lash up the bench from two adjacent stones.
Masonry branches off Knapping, in parallel with the kiln and pottery line. It is what lets a settlement build in stone — durable walls, stairs and foundations — once it has learned to dress the rock.
